hardware: c8a.4xlarge (AMD Zen5 EPYC 9R45, 16 vCPU) on-demand, ap-northeast-1
change: Two-step removal of the disassembly-verified 'entry check' tax in hamming(): (1) gate now tests BOTH slice lengths (a.len()==4 && b.len()==4) so the compiler can prove all 8 indexes in-range and drop the b-side bounds guards; (2) new `fixed256` cargo feature + typed hamming4(&[u64;4],&[u64;4]) compiles the width dispatch out entirely. Assembly before: ~6 of ~13 uops/call were length gate + bounds checks + panic-stub branches.
note: Recall bit-identical across all builds (same integers computed). The p50 drop is the same uop diet applied to the single-threaded latency pass, where dispatch pressure is undiluted by memory stalls. Cumulative at C=2000: 650 (067) -> 847 (069) -> 955 (071); C=500 crosses 1000 QPS at 10M on 16 vCPU. Dispatch-slot accounting predicted ~+20% in-cache from removing ~6/18 uops; measured +13% at the knee is consistent with the stream diluting kernel wins ~half.
